page: Microsoft Azure EC16ads_v6 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the ECadsv6 server family. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD processor and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU.

This server configuration includes 614 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, allowing for high-performance disk attachments. The instance does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses. There are no onboard graphics processing units, as the GPU count is zero.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory density relative to the 16 dedicated vCPUs positions this instance as a resource-efficient option for memory-bound workloads rather than compute-heavy operations. The primary tradeoff is the lack of specialized hardware accelerators like GPUs, which limits its utility for machine learning training. The hardware profile is designed for memory-intensive applications, database hosting, and enterprise workloads requiring substantial RAM and local temporary storage.
description: Microsoft Azure EC16ads_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the ECadsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD processor and 128.0 GB of system memory, establishing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Storage capabilities include 614 GB of local disk space alongside compatibility with Premium Storage. The instance does not feature any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This hardware profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads by providing a dense memory allocation relative to compute resources. The configuration is suited for database management, enterprise applications, and memory-bound data processing tasks where dedicated CPU execution and local temporary storage are required.
